title: Add Hundreds
description: Add the hundreds digits of two three-digit numbers.
condition: Two three-digit numbers are being added and their hundreds digits are identified.
response: Add the hundreds values to get a total number of hundreds.
label: Principle / Model

title: Add Ones
description: Add the ones digits of two numbers and determine the total number of ones.
condition: Two numbers are being added and their ones digits are identified.
response: Add the ones values to get a total number of ones.
label: Principle / Model

title: Add or Subtract Multiples of 10 or 100
description: Mentally adjust the tens or hundreds digit when adding or subtracting 10 or 100.
condition: A three-digit number plus or minus a multiple of 10 or 100.
response: Increase or decrease the tens or hundreds digit accordingly.
label: Principle

title: Add Tens
description: Add the tens digits of two numbers and determine the total number of tens.
condition: Two numbers are being added and their tens digits are identified.
response: Add the tens values to get a total number of tens.
label: Principle / Model

title: Add with Regrouping by Place Value
description: Compose a ten or hundred when the sum of ones or tens is 10 or more during addition.
condition: Addition of multi-digit numbers where ones or tens sum to 10 or more.
response: Compose a new ten or hundred and adjust the place-value totals accordingly.
label: Principle
example: For $428 + 175$: $8+5=13$ so write 3 ones and compose 1 ten; combine tens to make a new hundred, giving $603$.

title: Combine Place-Value Totals
description: Combine the totals of hundreds, tens, and ones to form the final sum.
condition: Totals for hundreds, tens, and ones have been determined (after any composing).
response: Add the place-value totals to state the final number.
label: Principle / Model

title: Compute Difference Within 1,000
description: Subtract one three-digit number from another, possibly requiring regrouping.
condition: Given a subtraction expression with two numbers within 1,000 (e.g., 421 − 139).
response: Compute and state the correct difference.
label: Production / Skill

title: Decompose a Hundred into Ten Tens
description: Regroup one hundred as ten tens when there are not enough tens to subtract.
condition: In subtraction, the tens digit of the minuend is smaller than the tens digit of the subtrahend after accounting for ones regrouping.
response: Trade 1 hundred for 10 tens, increasing the tens place by 10 and decreasing the hundreds place by 1.
label: Principle / Model
example: In $421 - 139$, after regrouping a ten, 1 ten is not enough to subtract 3 tens, so trade 1 hundred for 10 tens: $4$ hundreds becomes $3$ hundreds and $1$ ten becomes $11$ tens.
notes: Based on place-value equivalence: $1$ hundred = $10$ tens.

title: Find Missing Addend to 1,000
description: Determine the number that must be added to a given number to make 1,000.
condition: Equation of the form $a + \square = 1000$ or $\square + a = 1000$.
response: Subtract the known addend from 1,000 to find the missing number.
label: Principle

title: Group Addends to Make Friendly Sums
description: Reorder and group addends to form tens or hundreds to simplify addition.
condition: Sum of three or more numbers where some pairs make 10 or 100.
response: Rearrange and group numbers to form friendly sums before adding remaining values.
label: Principle
notes: Uses commutative and associative properties implicitly.

title: Identify Equivalent Numerical Expressions
description: Determine whether two numerical expressions have the same value.
condition: Given two addition or subtraction expressions.
response: Evaluate or mentally compare both expressions and determine if their values are equal.
label: Concept
notes: Response is a fixed judgment (equal/not equal) for each comparison.

title: Interpret Base-Ten Block Values
description: Identify the number represented by hundreds flats, tens rods, and ones cubes in a diagram.
condition: Given a base-ten block diagram showing hundreds, tens, and ones.
response: State the total value represented by the blocks.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Requires knowledge that a flat = 100, a rod = 10, a unit cube = 1.

title: Match Diagram to Subtraction Expression
description: Translate a base-ten diagram with crossed-out units into the corresponding subtraction expression.
condition: Given a base-ten diagram where certain hundreds, tens, or ones are crossed out to show removal.
response: Write or select the subtraction expression that matches the initial quantity and the amount removed.
label: Principle / Model
example: A diagram showing $434$ with $72$ crossed out corresponds to $434 - 72$.
notes: Requires identifying minuend as total shown and subtrahend as crossed-out amount.

title: Recognize Subtraction Is Not Commutative
description: Understand that changing the order of numbers in subtraction changes the result.
condition: Given two numbers $a$ and $b$ in a subtraction context.
response: Maintain correct order (minuend − subtrahend) and do not reverse them.
label: Concept
notes: Highlighted by distractor choice $72 + 434$ and discussion of commutative property.

title: Subtract Numbers by Place Value
description: Subtract multi-digit numbers by subtracting corresponding place values when no regrouping is required.
condition: Two multi-digit numbers (within 1,000) presented for subtraction without need for decomposition/regrouping.
response: Subtract hundreds, tens, and ones separately; combine the partial differences.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Valid when each place can be subtracted without regrouping.

title: Use Compensation (Add-On) Strategy for Subtraction
description: Find a difference by adding up from the subtrahend to the minuend using friendly numbers.
condition: Subtraction problem where counting up to a friendly number (ten or hundred) is efficient.
response: Add in parts to reach the minuend and sum the partial addends to find the difference.
label: Principle
